message:
bd-17s7: Traceback source_context for deterministic testing

Add `source_context` and `source_first_line` to TracebackFrame to allow
embedding source snippets directly instead of reading from filesystem.

Changes:
- TracebackFrame gains source_context() builder method
- Renderer prioritizes provided source over file reading
- 9 new unit tests for source context functionality
- Update conformance test parser to support new fields
- Update FEATURE_PARITY.md to reflect code context support

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-11zc","title":"demo_showcase tests: e2e harness + timeout runner","description":"Build a reusable integration test harness that spawns `demo_showcase` with timeouts, captures output, and logs diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:01.710821962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:37.078687371Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11zc","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:37.078658697Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-11zc","depends_on_id":"bd-2sk1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:01.752771403Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":66,"issue_id":"bd-11zc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Harness requirements

Implement a small test helper (Rust, in `tests/` or a shared module) that can:
- locate the compiled binary via `env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_demo_showcase")`
- run with specific args + env overrides
- capture stdout/stderr
- enforce a hard timeout (kill the process)
- log diagnostics using `test-log` + `tracing`

## Diagnostic logging

On every run log:
- full argv
- env overrides
- start/stop timestamps + duration
- exit status
- byte counts for stdout/stderr

On failure, additionally log:
- the first/last N lines of stdout/stderr (truncate to avoid CI spam)

## Acceptance criteria

- A single helper function makes it easy to write new e2e tests without duplication.
- Timeouts are enforced reliably (no hanging CI).

 {"id":"bd-12nv","title":"demo_showcase: non-interactive safety policy","description":"Specify TTY/non-TTY behavior rules and ensure no prompts/animations run when piped.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:18.059944267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:20.111288380Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:34.024208222Z","close_reason":"Completed (TTY/non-TTY safety policy specified in README demo_showcase section)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-12nv","depends_on_id":"bd-23h8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:35.899375451Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-12nv","depends_on_id":"bd-sowt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:18.075242767Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":18,"issue_id":"bd-12nv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Policy

Define hard rules so the demo is safe everywhere:

- If stdout is not a TTY and `--force-terminal` is not set:
  - disable live refresh
  - disable alt-screen
  - disable prompt/pager
  - print static snapshots only
- If interactive is disabled explicitly (`--no-interactive`):
  - skip prompts
  - do not rely on keypresses to proceed

## Acceptance criteria

- No scene may require user input to terminate.
- No infinite loops; all animation is time-bounded and/or gated on TTY.
- Output remains readable when ANSI is suppressed.

 {"id":"bd-164e","title":"demo_showcase: export bundle (HTML/SVG)","description":"Add optional HTML/SVG export of the demo output (capture + write files) with clear UX and paths.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:45.512659356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:47.156286674Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","export"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-164e","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:47.156258251Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-164e","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:45.540309392Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"bd-164e","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Scope

Support a flag that captures the demo output and writes an export bundle:
- `demo_showcase.html` (inline or external css)
- `demo_showcase.svg`

The export is an important “proof of portability”: output can be embedded in docs, GitHub issues, etc.

## UX expectations

- `--export-dir <path>` writes files there and prints a final summary panel with the paths.
- `--export` writes to a temp dir (like the existing `examples/e2e_export.rs`) and prints the dir.
- Export should be deterministic when `--width/--height` are provided.

## Acceptance criteria

- Export works with `--features full,tracing` (and without).
- The exported HTML/SVG contains representative content (not just a trivial “hello”).
- The demo prints clear instructions for viewing the artifacts.

 {"id":"bd-2sk1","title":"demo_showcase: automated test suite (unit + e2e + snapshots)","description":"Add comprehensive unit + integration + snapshot tests for demo_showcase (with detailed logging) to prevent regressions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:09:38.949498076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:09:51.444835092Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","qa","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2sk1","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:09:38.968252493Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":65,"issue_id":"bd-2sk1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Why this exists

The showcase is meant to be a *flagship* artifact, so it needs flagship testing:
- Output regressions are subtle and easy to introduce during refactors.
- Live/TTY behavior can break silently without automated checks.
- Export artifacts (HTML/SVG) are particularly prone to drift.

## Testing philosophy

We want three layers:

1. **Unit tests (fast, internal)**
   - CLI parsing, config defaults, scene registry ordering
   - small pure helpers (badges/formatting)

2. **Integration/e2e smoke tests (black-box)**
   - Spawn the compiled binary via `CARGO_BIN_EXE_demo_showcase`
   - Run each scene with CI-safe flags (`--no-live --no-screen --no-interactive --quick`)
   - Assert exit code, bounded runtime (timeout), and key output markers

3. **Snapshot/golden tests (visual regression)**
   - Capture deterministic text output (fixed `--width/--height`, `--color-system none` or ANSI-stripped)
   - Use `insta` snapshots for key scenes

## Logging requirements

All integration tests should emit structured debug logs (via `test-log` + `tracing`) including:
- command line executed
- env overrides
- runtime duration
- output sizes
- first/last N lines on failure (without spamming CI)

## Acceptance criteria

- `cargo test` passes quickly and runs unit + basic smoke tests.
- `cargo test --features full,tracing` runs the full snapshot suite (including content scenes).
- E2E tests fail with actionable logs when a scene hangs or output changes unexpectedly.

-{"id":"bd-2smc","title":"demo_showcase: dashboard layout + live updates","description":"Build the core split-screen dashboard (Layout) and drive it with Live updates (progress/logs).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:36.694609635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:34:54.652303002Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","layout","live"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:30.240263939Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:27.173009186Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:36.713272217Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-2smc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Dashboard concept

Create a single “mission control” dashboard that stays on screen and updates live:
- **Header**: app name + run id + clock + key status badges
- **Main**: pipeline progress (multi-step), current action panel
- **Sidebar**: service status table + quick facts
- **Footer**: streaming logs (tail) + hints (“press q”, etc.)

This should be the primary wow-moment: layout + live refresh + dense styling.

## Technical approach

- Model a `DemoState` struct (progress values, current step, log ring buffer, service status, etc.).
- Use `Live::get_renderable(|| Box<dyn Renderable>)` with a closure that builds a fresh `Layout` from the current state snapshot.
- Default to `LiveOptions { screen: true, ... }` when interactive; provide flags to disable.
- Keep refresh rate modest (e.g. 10 fps max) and driven by `LiveOptions.refresh_per_second`.

## Acceptance criteria

- In an interactive terminal, the dashboard updates smoothly and exits cleanly (cursor restored; no leftover alt-screen unless `--screen`).
- In non-interactive mode, a static “snapshot” dashboard is printed once (no live loop).

+{"id":"bd-2smc","title":"demo_showcase: dashboard layout + live updates","description":"Build the core split-screen dashboard (Layout) and drive it with Live updates (progress/logs).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:36.694609635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:38:49.077869634Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","layout","live"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-1388","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:38:49.077815984Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:30.240263939Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:27.173009186Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:36.713272217Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-2smc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Dashboard concept

Create a single “mission control” dashboard that stays on screen and updates live:
- **Header**: app name + run id + clock + key status badges
- **Main**: pipeline progress (multi-step), current action panel
- **Sidebar**: service status table + quick facts
- **Footer**: streaming logs (tail) + hints (“press q”, etc.)

This should be the primary wow-moment: layout + live refresh + dense styling.

## Technical approach

- Model a `DemoState` struct (progress values, current step, log ring buffer, service status, etc.).
- Use `Live::get_renderable(|| Box<dyn Renderable>)` with a closure that builds a fresh `Layout` from the current state snapshot.
- Default to `LiveOptions { screen: true, ... }` when interactive; provide flags to disable.
- Keep refresh rate modest (e.g. 10 fps max) and driven by `LiveOptions.refresh_per_second`.

## Acceptance criteria

- In an interactive terminal, the dashboard updates smoothly and exits cleanly (cursor restored; no leftover alt-screen unless `--screen`).
- In non-interactive mode, a static “snapshot” dashboard is printed once (no live loop).
- Layout adapts to narrow terminals: if width < threshold, collapse sidebar or switch to single-column mode.

 {"id":"bd-3m5v","title":"demo_showcase: end-to-end showcase binary (turned up to 11)","description":"Standalone `demo_showcase` binary that showcases rich_rust end-to-end in a cohesive, polished, real-world narrative (all features, turned up to 11).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:20.204349495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:03.129344912Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase"],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-3m5v","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Vision

Create a **standalone Rust binary** (`cargo run --bin demo_showcase`) that feels like a real production CLI app, but uses rich_rust to an almost ridiculous degree: polished, stylish, visually dense, and clearly useful.

The goal is not a scattered “feature zoo”. It should read like a cohesive product demo (think: deploy pipeline / ops dashboard / release assistant), with a narrative flow, sensible UI hierarchy, and a few “wow” moments.

## Why this matters

- Proves feature completeness (tables/panels/layout/live/logging/export/etc.) by exercising *everything together* under realistic constraints.
- Provides a high-signal artifact for future refactors (human eyeballs notice regressions immediately).
- Makes the library compelling to adopt: it demonstrates *usefulness*, not just correctness.

## High-level demo flow (expected experience)

1. **Hero screen**: branded title, palette/theme preview, environment capability panel (TTY, color system, width/height).
2. **Dashboard** (Layout): split-screen UI with header + sidebar + main pane.
3. **Live run**: progress bars/spinners + streaming logs; deliberate use of markup, emojis, hyperlinks, and named theme styles.
4. **Deep dives**: Markdown (release notes), Syntax (config/code), JSON (request/response), Pretty/Inspect (structs), Traceback (synthetic error path).
5. **Export**: optional HTML/SVG export bundle (captures output and writes files) with clear paths + instructions.
6. **Wrap-up**: summary table/tree + next steps.

## Non-negotiable constraints

- **Must not hang** in non-interactive contexts (CI / piped output).
- Live/alt-screen/interactive prompts must auto-disable when not a TTY (or via flags).
- Default run should be reasonably quick (configurable via `--speed` / `--quick`).
- Must compile and run cleanly with `--features full,tracing` (and degrade gracefully when features are missing).
- No `unsafe` code anywhere.

## Acceptance criteria (Epic-level)

- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase --features full,tracing` produces a cohesive, impressive end-to-end demo that clearly exercises: markup/styles/themes, tables, panels, rules, trees, columns/align/padding, layout engine, live updates, progress/spinners, logging (+ tracing if enabled), pretty/inspect, traceback, emoji replacement, hyperlinks, and HTML/SVG export.
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase` (no features) still runs without crashing; optional scenes display friendly “compile with …” guidance.
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase | cat` completes without interactive waits and without runaway ANSI control spam.

## Implementation notes

- Keep the binary self-contained: no network, no external assets required.
- Prefer a small hand-rolled CLI parser (avoid heavy deps).
- Treat this as a *product demo*: strong defaults + flags for power users (`--no-live`, `--screen`, `--export-dir`, etc.).
- Use `Theme` and named styles heavily so the output demonstrates the theming system in a way users can copy.
